results.rationale: The polished title is a single-article rendering and conflicts with the established Chinese name for Laufey. Keep it as an observation until a source-context match resolves whether this is a distinct title or an inconsistent rendering.
results.refined_rule: Observe the title rendering for God of War Laufey; do not add it to the dictionary without entity confirmation.
counterexamples: Existing usage maps Laufey to 劳菲 rather than retaining Laufey in English.
results.misuse_risk: Could create an incorrect dictionary entry for a title or character reference.

results.rationale: The user consistently changed this article to 崛起：罗马之子, but the candidate pair itself is only supported by one article on one day. Retain it for future corroboration rather than promoting it to the dictionary.
results.refined_rule: Observe Ryse: Son of Rome → 崛起：罗马之子 pending corroborating user-polished examples or explicit feedback.
results.misuse_risk: A single article-level normalization may not establish the intended canonical game title.

results.rationale: The English source phrase is a role description, not the game title. Mapping it to Fallout: New Vegas drops the person/role information and creates an invalid entity pair.
results.refined_rule: Do not create a dictionary candidate from a title phrase when its English anchor includes a role description rather than the named work alone.
counterexamples: New Vegas Lead Designer refers to a person’s role, while Fallout: New Vegas is a game title.
results.misuse_risk: Would cause role phrases to be replaced by game titles and corrupt factual meaning.

results.rationale: Deborah Ann Woll is the actor named in the English title; the Chinese title identifies the God of War: Laufey project. These are not the same entity.
results.refined_rule: Reject candidate pairs that map a person’s name to a work title without an explicit person-name translation.
counterexamples: Deborah Ann Woll is an actor; God of War: Laufey is a game/project title.
results.misuse_risk: Would replace personal names with unrelated titles in future translations.
